Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: looking at the events, we see three distinct sets of patient demographics: 1. patient_age: 45, patient_gender: M (appears in events 1,4,7,12,15) 2. patient_age: 62, patient_gender: F (appears in events 2,5,8,10,11,14,17) 3. patient_age: 28, patient_gender: M (appears in events 3,6,9,13,16,18)

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: But we also have department information. For the 45M patient, all events are in ER or Radiology (which is likely a service for ER). For the 62F patient, all events are in ER or Laboratory (which is a service). For the 28M patient, all events are in Surgery.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also, the activities seem to follow a logical order for each patient: - For 45M: Register patient (ER) -> Initial triage (ER) -> X-ray imaging (Radiology) -> Doctor consultation (ER) -> Patient discharged (ER) - For 62F: Register patient (ER) -> Initial triage (ER) -> Lab test requested (ER) -> Lab test completed (Laboratory) -> Doctor consultation (ER) -> Prescription created (ER) -> Patient discharged (ER) - For 28M: Register patient (Surgery) -> Doctor consultation (Surgery) -> Surgery preparation (Surgery) -> Surgery performed (Surgery) -> Recovery room (Surgery) -> Patient discharged (Surgery)

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: However, we must check if any patient could have multiple registers? Possibly not. The timestamps are all on the same day and within a short time frame, so likely three separate patients.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We should also consider if any event could belong to more than one case? Unlikely because the patient_age and patient_gender are consistent within each group.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus, we can assign case IDs based on patient demographics and department consistency. Lets list events per case: Case 1 (45M, ER): - Event 1: Register patient, ER - Event 4: Initial triage, ER - Event 7: X-ray imaging, Radiology (but for the same patient, likely linked to ER visit) - Event 12: Doctor consultation, ER (diagnosis=hairline_fracture) - Event 15: Patient discharged, ER Case 2 (62F, ER): - Event 2: Register patient, ER - Event 5: Initial triage, ER - Event 8: Lab test requested, ER - Event 10: Lab test completed, Laboratory (but for the same patient) - Event 11: Doctor consultation, ER (diagnosis=minor_infection) - Event 14: Prescription created, ER - Event 17: Patient discharged, ER Case 3 (28M, Surgery): - Event 3: Register patient, Surgery - Event 6: Doctor consultation, Surgery - Event 9: Surgery preparation, Surgery - Event 13: Surgery performed, Surgery - Event 16: Recovery room, Surgery - Event 18: Patient discharged, Surgery

Name: Validation - PE
Text: All events are accounted for. We can also note that the timestamps are in chronological order overall, but interleaved. The grouping by patient demographics yields a coherent timeline for each patient.
